title: Vesicles on Hands and Mouth - HFMD
chief_complaint: 3-year-old with fever 38.5°C, sore throat, drooling, and painful oral ulcers for 2 days. Today developed vesicular rash on palms and soles.

physical_exam.mouth: Multiple small vesicles and ulcers on tongue, buccal mucosa, and soft palate
physical_exam.skin: Small oval vesicles on palms, soles, and between fingers, some on buttocks. Non-pruritic.

correct_diagnosis: Hand, Foot, and Mouth Disease (Coxsackie A16)
key_learning_points: Vesicles on hands + feet + mouth = HFMD

patient_responses.eating: He refuses to eat because his mouth hurts
patient_responses.fever: Fever for 2 days, not too high
patient_responses.rash: The blisters appeared today on his hands and feet
patient_responses.daycare: Other kids at daycare have similar symptoms

feedback.incorrect_message: This is HFMD. The vesicles on hands, feet, and mouth are pathognomonic.
feedback.attending_note: HFMD management: Supportive care - acetaminophen/ibuprofen for pain and fever. Encourage cold fluids, popsicles. Magic mouthwash (Maalox + Benadryl) for severe oral pain. Return if dehydration (no urine >8h). Exclude from daycare until fever-free and vesicles crusted.
